feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Implementing Address Typeahead using SAP Data Quality Services for SuccessFactors Address portlet
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

We are in process of implementing Address Typeahead solution utilizing SAP BTP service SAP Data Quality Management, microservices for location data in SuccessFactors Employee Central Address Information. We are doing some impact assessment before starting the implementation, to check whether this solution will fit for Ericsson landscape or not. We have few queries, need to your help to clarify the same.

 

Q1. For Address Typeahead, The JSON file (SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central - Standard Typeahead Configuration - BEL BRA DEU NLD USA file) with the preconfigured local version mappings was offered by SAP to kick start the implementation. But this file only provide configuration standard data model delivered by SAP for the home address in the country/region specific Succession Data Model (CSF SDM). For our customer, standard configurations were not followed, we have custom configurations in Home address. Is it possible to do this kind of custom configurations in SAP DQM Address Typeahead services by following custom configurations ? I have attached the screenshot of configurations of USA country for your reference.

 

Q2. We have more than 100+ countries configured in our customer SF environment, do we need to do the individual country wise separate mapping in SAP DQM Address Typeahead services configurations? or if few countries follows same configurations then can we utilize the 'default' mapping, instead of country specific separate mapping in SAP DQM services?

 


Q3. We would like to utilize the Address Typeahead solution for all countries, so if we enable the service in SuccessFactors provisioning "Employee Central Address Validation Service" under company setting then this will be enough ? or do we need to configure the 'Address Validation Configuration For Country/Region' under manage data for all countries separately?

 

Q4. How to transport the mapping configurations from one to another environment in SAP DQM? Or do we need to manually configure everything in higher environment again?

entry.title: SAP BTP Cockpit free trail account - error "Service" broker
entry.content:
I am encountering an issue when attempting to execute the boost, which consistently fails with the following error message:

Service broker error: "Service" broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: "Forbidden"

In addition to this, I have attempted the following troubleshooting steps:

  1. Manually creating an instance of the service.
  2. Re-creating the sub-account associated with the service.

Unfortunately, neither of these approaches resolved the issue, and the error persists.

Does anyone happen to know what it could be?

entry.title: Set Up Integration Suite Trial
entry.content:
Hello,

I created a new trial account  following this tutorial to have a SAP Business Application Studio subscription and having a Subscription Failed as you cans see on secreenshots.

Please How can i fix this? I look similar question but not having clear answer.

Thanks

entry.title: Enquiry about Joule activation using BTP Trail account
entry.content:
I am trying to install Joule AI tool on one of my SF demo systems given by my company. I took BTP Trial version with my registered s-user id & email .What I want to know is Can Joule be activated in Successfactors demo system using BTP Trail version?

I was unable to find Joule as a product in BTP? Can this be done?If No is there any workaround?

entry.title: Subscribe multitenant app in different global account
entry.content:
Hello experts,

we have a multitenant application hosted on SAP BTP which uses resources such as destination service and cloud connector. This works fine when our customers' subscribing subaccounts reside in the same global account that also keeps the subaccount that provides the application. I know this should be the preferred way to realize multitenant subscriptions. However, some customers would like to subscribe the app from within their own global account, i.e. a subaccount should subscribe to another subaccount in a different global account.

As described in this article service broker could be an option to do this but it comes with disadvantages (cloud connector/destinations cannot be used).

Is there another approach to subscribe to a multitenant app/subaccount in a different global account?

Many thanks in advance and kind regards,

Jane

Want to change domain and host on SAP BTP - IS - tenant

entry.title: Subscribe Path in Autoflow
entry.content:
I create Autoflow in SAP Sales Cloud V2 for Sales Order in order to create data from sales cloud v2 to s4hana public cloud system via Integration Suite.

I would like to know which path should be entered for Subscribe Path.

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Not able to subscribe to SAP Business Application Studio using my BTP trial account
entry.content:
Not able to subscribe to SAP Business Application Studio using my BTP trial account.

Have tried multiple times by deleting the subscription request and re-subscribing to SAP Business Application Studio.

entry.title: Insufficient Resources Error while deploying in CF (BTP Trial)
entry.content:
Good morning,

My team and I have been working with the trial account for some time, developing applications and running tests without any issues. However, starting today, whenever we try to start the applications deployed in CF, they become stuck and never fully initialize.
When attempting to deploy them using mbt build > cf deploy, we encounter the following error:

 

Error staging application "apptests-db-deployer": InsufficientResources - Insufficient resources

 

We’ve tried adjusting the memory and disk parameters to the minimum possible values, but the error persists.
We also tested this on different trial accounts, and the same issue occurs in all of them.

Does anyone have any idea what might be happening? Is this a known, general issue that will be resolved soon?

Please note that we’re working with an Amazon Web Services (AWS) account in the US East (VA) region.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Subscription Failed while creating SAP BAS Subscription
entry.content: Got this error while create a BTP Account and upon checking this error occured.

entry.title: When an API call is made to CAP metadata returns in json format
entry.content:
Hi 

When an API call is made to the CAP backend service it returns in json format but for our usecase we need it to return in xml . (we don't manage the api call so adding Accept -application/xml in the header is not possible).

entry.title: Cloud Foundry memory plan required for HTML5 apps?
entry.content:
Hello community,

I’d appreciate insights on whether CF memory assignment is essential in this specific scenario.

 

[Scencrio overview]

I’m working on developing and deploying a Fiori app to the managed approuter. Here's the approach I’ve followed:

  1. Develop a Fiori app in Build Code within the Dev subaccount.
  2. Prepare the Deploy subaccount by enabling Cloud Foundry (CF), subscribing to Build Work Zone (standard edition), and the XSUAA application plan.
  3. Deploy the Fiori app to the Deploy subaccount's HTML5 application repository, which is enabled via Build Work Zone.

This scenario is thoroughly explained in the mission "Get Started on SAP BTP with SAPUI5/Fiori - Create a Hello World App" available here: https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/3585/3628/ .

 

[Question]

The mission suggests assigning CF memory to the Deploy subaccount. However, I’m wondering if this step is truly necessary.

From my understanding, a Fiori app is a frontend application that the HTML5 application repository can serve. Without assigning CF memory, I was able to successfully deploy the app to the HTML5 Applications repository and run the app. The underlying services were also deployed to the CF space using the free dependent services.

Btw, I recognize that assigning CF memory is typically required for full-stack applications with a standalone app-router scenario, where the application itself is deployed to the CF space.

 

Regards
EunSeok

entry.title: Couldn't subscribe to Integration Suite.
entry.content:
please help me on the below error message while subscribing to integration suite

 

Couldn't subscribe to Integration Suite. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

entry.title: How to mashup and combine external entity with custom entity for BTP CAP?
entry.content:
I have constructed SBS application which consumes the external service data using destination from SaaS SAP system. Now I would like to use some custom properties of my own together with ready entity definition from external service. Unfortunately, I have error when I try to combine. Using "extend" or "extend projection" does not work either.

Is there anyway how to fix this issue? Thanks.

Entity definition:

entity getPromoCustom as select from ex.Promotions {
    key promotionID,
    promotionName,
};
extend getPromoCustom with {
    salesOrg   : String(4);
    distChain  : String(4);
};
 
Error:
Use ‘extend projection’ to add select items to the query entityCDS (compiler)(extend-query)

entry.title: Why the error loop on a Fiori Elements application when running on SAP Work Zone(Launchpad)
entry.content:
I wrote a CAP application with a Fiori Elements, business as usual.

When writing an event handler, I wanted to reject, return an error, if certain condition is met:

this.on('READ', 'MyEntity', async (req) => {
  // ...
  if (!filter) {
    req.reject(500,'Please select a filter.');
  }
  // ...
  return result;
});

What happens is that, if I run the application standalone, locally, or in BTP's HTML5 Repository, it behaves correctly. When an error happens it returns the error and stops. I fix the input and the app runs fine:

One error

 

However, if I run it on SAP WorkZone, standard edition (former Launchpad), either in BTP or using the cds-launchpad-plugin, it enters in an infinite error loop:

Infinite errors

and the list keeps growing. If I close the error screen it immediately re-opens and keeps outputting more errors. The only way to stop it is to exit the page.

To share some light on what's happening, it keeps sending the same "wrong" request. If I put a breakpoint in my application it will hold and stop the loop. But as soon as I release it, the front-end fails and sends the same request again.

The workaround I had to do was to catch the error and return a valid response:

try {
  //...
} catch (e) {
  // throw e; // doesn't work
  // req.reject(e.message); // doesn't work
  console.error(e.message); // log for information
  // return a valid object, but fill the values with the error information
  return [{
    title: "ERROR",
    description: e.message
  }];
}

 

Let me know if you faced something similar and if you know how to stop this infinite loop.

entry.title: How to Edit and Annotate an Image in SAP MDK?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I have a requirement in SAP MDK where an image is displayed on a mdk page, and I need to:

  1. Edit the image directly within the app.
  2. Add inline annotations, such as text or highlights, on top of the image.

Is there any standard control or feature in SAP MDK to achieve this functionality? If not, are there any compatible NativeScript plugins or workarounds to implement this in SAP MDK?

Any guidance, documentation links, or examples would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you,
Divan Wellalage
